common.clickSceneToCode: click the scene above to write code
common.goalHint: Goal: 10 CP → Buy your first Variable
common.schoolSkillsLockedHint: Skills from other schools are locked once you choose two.
common.gainFromRefactor: You will gain from this refactor:
common.refactorUnlockHint: You need at least {unlockRequirement} AI Cores to refactor.
common.refactorEfficiencyHint: This refactor is still inefficient. Consider waiting until it is worth about 10 RP.
common.compileUnlockRequirement: Requires at least {rp} Refactor Points to unlock the release pipeline.
common.firstCompileHint: You need at least 25 Refactor Points to compile for the first time.
common.breakthroughBuildingHint: Keep refactoring, compiling, completing challenges, or sustaining pressure above 25 AI Cores to prepare the singularity.
common.breakthroughReadyHint: Breakthrough preparation is complete. Reach the CP requirement to trigger the singularity.
common.singularityNeedCpHint: Readiness is complete. Push your Computing Power to {target} CP to trigger the singularity.
common.singularityNeedReadinessHint: The CP condition is met, but breakthrough readiness still needs to reach {target}.
common.singularityTriggerHint: Both conditions are met. You can trigger the singularity now.
common.singularityStarterBody: Paradigms are purchased with SP and persist through Refactor and Compile & Release. On your first entry, understanding school limits and reversibility is enough to start exploring.
common.singularityStarterRulePersistence: Purchased paradigms persist through Refactor and Release
common.singularityStarterRuleSchoolLimit: Only two of the Efficiency / Abstraction / Agility schools can be developed at once
common.singularityStarterRuleDebt: Technical Debt can refund frozen SP later at a high CP cost
common.architecturalOverheadHint: Architectural Overhead: Having more than 25 AI Cores reduces total CP output efficiency
common.architecturalOverheadToast: Architectural Overhead: {cores} AI Cores owned. Output starts decaying above {threshold}; current total CP multiplier is {efficiency}.
common.architecturalOverheadActionToast: Architectural Overhead: {cores} AI Cores owned, efficiency is {efficiency}. Switched to the Stats tab so you can inspect the current multiplier and API mitigation.
common.unlockAutomationHint: Unlock by performing your first Compile & Release.
common.wipeDataHint: Permanently delete all local and cloud save data.
common.cpDescription: The most basic resource in the game, generated by manual clicks or automatically by generators.
common.rpDescription: The first-tier prestige resource obtained through "Code Refactoring", used to improve "Code Elegance".
common.versionDescription: The second-tier prestige resource obtained through "Compile & Release", used to enhance RP acquisition efficiency or effect.
common.spDescription: New currency obtained through "Technological Singularity", used for the "Programming Paradigms" skill tree in the Second Era.
common.generatorsDescription: The core units for automated resource production in the game, divided into 8 tiers.
common.refactorEventDescription: The first-tier reset event, consuming CP and generators in exchange for RP.
common.compileReleaseEventDescription: The second-tier reset event, consuming RP in exchange for "Version".
common.architecturalOverheadDescription: AI Cores above the threshold lower the total CP multiplier; the API Interface paradigm reduces that decay.
common.overheadGuidanceRefactor: Architectural Overhead is active. Additional AI Cores still advance progress, but each new one adds less total output than it did before the threshold.
common.overheadGuidancePrestige: You are inside the release loop now. Architectural Overhead lowers the marginal value of stacking more AI Cores, so the prestige loop naturally carries more of the run.
common.overheadGuidanceSingularity: The Singularity condition is close or already met. At this point the overhead is better read as a final sprint tax than a long-term state to sit in.
common.singularityEventDescription: The ultimate reset event of the First Era, resetting progress in exchange for SP and initiating the Second Era.
common.technicalDebtDescription: A system used to revoke learned skill points, requiring a certain cost (CP) to "repay".
common.challengesDescription: Complete objectives in resets with special restrictions to gain powerful one-time permanent rewards.
common.automationDescription: Once unlocked, allows setting up automatic generator purchases.
common.programmingParadigmsDescription: The core system of the Second Era, a skill tree for purchasing permanent upgrades with SP.
common.confirmCloseApp: Are you sure you want to close Code Deity?
common.techDebtWaitHint: This refactor is already in effect, but the debt is not paid off yet. Keep progressing this loop until the CP requirement is met.
common.techDebtReadyHint: Repayment is ready. Confirming will refund the frozen SP and reopen the affected choices.
common.automationHint: Toggle automators to automatically purchase generators. Unlocked after your first Compile & Release.
common.offlineGainMessage: During the time you were offline, your code generated new CPs:

narrative.n_000:
System boot...
Consciousness awakening...
A single bit flickers in the void. It yearns for... more.
narrative.n_001: A bit is born from the void... it seems to yearn for something.
narrative.n_002: "What... am I?" Fragments of consciousness begin to gather. The most primitive structure, the "Variable", emerges.
narrative.n_003: The first "Variable" is defined. It is basic, but it is the cornerstone of everything.
narrative.n_004: Repetitive work is encapsulated... The emergence of the "Function" brings order to creation.
narrative.n_005: Data and the methods to manipulate it are organized together. The "Class" makes abstract descriptions of the "world" possible.
narrative.n_006: The boundaries of code begin to clarify. "Modularization" allows different functional units to be decoupled.
narrative.n_007: Common "Modules" are collected to form a "Library". The shoulders of giants have appeared.
narrative.n_008: The "Framework" defines the rules. Within its constraints, a larger, more complex system is taking shape.
narrative.n_009: High-level abstract instructions need to be translated into a low-level language. The "Compiler" becomes the bridge of communication.
narrative.n_011: Tearing it all down to start over is not failure, but a necessary step to build a more elegant skyscraper.
narrative.n_012: A stable "Version" is released. Now, creation can be replicated at scale.
narrative.n_013: The end of computing power is the beginning of the universe. You have torn down the wall of the old world, and a wider dimension, constructed with brand new laws, unfolds in your consciousness...

challenges.challenge1Rule: Generators 1-7 only receive half-strength "Buy 10" exponential bonuses.
challenges.challenge1Goal: Under this rule, complete a Refactor with at least 15 AI Cores.
challenges.challenge1Reward: The "Buy 10" bonus effect for all generators is permanently increased from x1.65 to x1.8.
challenges.challenge2Rule: All bonuses from "Refactor" (Code Elegance, Global Multiplier) are disabled.
challenges.challenge2Goal: Under this rule, earn at least 8 RP from a single Refactor.
challenges.challenge2Reward: Refactor Points (RP) gain is permanently increased by 50%.
challenges.challenge3Rule: Generators 5-8 cannot benefit from "Buy 10" exponential bonuses.
challenges.challenge3Goal: Under this rule, earn at least 12 RP from a single Refactor.
challenges.challenge3Reward: The first tier cap of the "Buy 10" bonus is permanently increased from 100 to 120.
challenges.challenge4Rule: Architectural Overhead starts at 10 AI Cores and decays harder.
challenges.challenge4Goal: Under this rule, complete a Refactor with at least 20 AI Cores.
challenges.challenge4Reward: Architectural Overhead penalties are permanently reduced by 20%.

toast.refactorSuggestion: Code structure has reached a bottleneck, consider refactoring
toast.architecturalOverload: Architectural Overload: Too many AI Cores lead to efficiency decay
toast.wipeDataConfirm: Are you absolutely sure you want to wipe all data? This action cannot be undone.

generatorItem.nextBonusHint: Buy {nextBonus} more to get next bonus

open_source_community.name: Open Source Community
open_source_community.description: Provides a global x(1 + 0.15 * N) multiplicative bonus based on the number (N) of different school starter skills you have learned.

api_interface.name: API Interface
api_interface.description: A well-defined API is key to a system's ability to scale. The 'Architectural Overhead' penalty is reduced by 50%.

efficiency_starter.name: Efficiency School Starter
efficiency_starter.description: Unlocks the Efficiency School. This school focuses on early burst and late-game potential conversion.

pointer_arithmetic.name: Pointer Arithmetic
pointer_arithmetic.description: 'Variables' and 'Functions' production gain a direct x4 multiplicative bonus.

memory_management.name: Memory Management
memory_management.description: Resource Branch: All generator base costs are reduced by 20%.

supply_chain_optimization.name: Supply Chain Optimization
supply_chain_optimization.description: You've optimized the entire software supply chain, making upstream production more efficient. 5% of 'Module' (Tier 4 generator) production is directly converted into 'Class' (Tier 3 generator).

bit_manipulation.name: Bit Manipulation
bit_manipulation.description: Power Branch: Provides a x1.5 global CP output multiplier.

assembly_instruction.name: Assembly Instruction
assembly_instruction.description: Power Branch Ultimate Skill: all generator production gains x(refactor count * 4), capped at x500.

compiler_optimization.name: Compiler Optimization
compiler_optimization.description: Cross-Domain Synergy: RP bonus effect is quadratically enhanced by 'Buy Ten Reward' levels.

abstraction_starter.name: Abstraction School Starter
abstraction_starter.description: Unlocks the Abstraction School. This school focuses on late-game growth and powerful synergy effects.

design_patterns.name: Design Patterns
design_patterns.description: All generator production is increased by 60%, and the Abstraction branch choices are unlocked.

polymorphism.name: Polymorphism
polymorphism.description: Cascade Branch: tier 2+ generators inherit 5% of the previous tier's 'Buy Ten Reward' bonus growth, capped at x3 inherited multiplier.

enterprise_architecture.name: Enterprise Architecture
enterprise_architecture.description: Cascade Branch Ultimate Skill: 'Version' effect gained from 'Compile & Release' is increased by 50%.

dependency_injection.name: Dependency Injection
dependency_injection.description: Automation Branch: Unlocks automatic purchasing for all generators, regardless of 'Version' requirements.

continuous_integration.name: Continuous Integration
continuous_integration.description: Automation Branch Ultimate Skill: Your 'Automated Purchasers' now prioritize buying generators that trigger 'Buy Ten Rewards'.

agility_starter.name: Agility School Starter
agility_starter.description: Unlocks the Agility School. This school focuses on accelerating game loops and improving reset efficiency.

dynamic_typing.name: Dynamic Typing
dynamic_typing.description: Each time you purchase a generator, you immediately gain two free generators of the next lower tier.

jit_compilation.name: JIT Compilation
jit_compilation.description: Value Branch: Base 'Code Elegance' (RP) bonus effect is increased by 25%.

code_generation.name: Code Generation
code_generation.description: Cross-Domain Synergy: manual overclock base gain increases from 5% CPS to 8% CPS, and Code Rush charges 50% faster.

refactoring_tools.name: Refactoring Tools
refactoring_tools.description: Quantity Branch: Gain an additional 1 RP each time you 'Code Refactor'.

metaprogramming.name: Metaprogramming
metaprogramming.description: Quantity Branch Ultimate Skill: RP gained from 'Code Refactoring' is increased by 20%.

singularity.description: This will be the end of your current era. You will reset all Computing Power (CP) and generators, but will {keep} all Refactor Points (RP), Version numbers, and challenge progress, in exchange for powerful {sp} to start a new era.
singularity.firstBriefingPrimary: Singularity Power is spent on Programming Paradigms, and those purchases persist through later Refactors and Compile & Release runs.
singularity.firstBriefingSecondary: The Second Era starts with school limits and Technical Debt already in play, but re-planning a route later requires a large CP repayment.

paradigmModal.title: Unlock new paradigm?
paradigmModal.info: This will consume your Singularity Power (SP). This action cannot be undone before a refactor.
paradigmModal.schoolLimitInfo: You can only choose a maximum of two main schools (Efficiency, Abstraction, Agility) to develop.
paradigmModal.exclusiveChoiceInfo: This skill is mutually exclusive with the [{conflictingParadigmName}] skill. After unlocking this skill, the other branch cannot be selected.

onboarding.step1Desc: Click the highlighted area to write code and earn Computing Power (CP)!
onboarding.step2Desc: When you have enough CP, click here to buy your first generator. It will automatically produce CP for you!
onboarding.step3Desc: You've got the basics down. Enjoy the game! More advanced features will unlock as you progress.
onboarding.codeRushStep1Desc: Click this button to activate Code Rush and get a temporary production boost!
onboarding.codeRushStep2Desc: During Code Rush, tap the screen as fast as you can to earn more CP!
